China's clampdown Bitcoin continues, virtual currency's prices keep plunging.
early morning Wednesday, price Bitcoin went down around $500 across major exchanges, lowest nearly month.
weeks ago, China's central bank said Bitcoin currency thus prohibited local financial institutions from processing bitcoin transaction.
Now, central bank banned third-party payment companies from dealing bitcoins, reported multiple news outlets.
result these regulations, which into question viability Bitcoin currency China, global price Bitcoin dipped less than $500, after $1,000 just three weeks ago.
chart below, from Blockchain.info, shows plunge Bitcoin price result China's clampdown.
Tuesday, People's Bank China asked largest Chinese third-party payment companies, which account half online transactions China, stop dealing Bitcoin, according local news reports, move that will effectively block deposits bitcoin into local exchanges.
China, world's largest Bitcoin exchange, confirmed Wednesday with post official Sina Weibo account that would longer accept deposits Chinese currency yuan, result latest ban.
"For reasons know, China cease renminbi [yuan] account charging functions," China announced, according York Times.
China's co-founder Bobby later confirmed news Financial Times.
right now, have received notice from third-party payment company that they will disallow customers from making deposits into exchange," said.
announcement sent price Bitcoin down $200 less than hours, Techinasia reported.
With financial institutions banned from processing bitcoin transactions with this bitcoin deposits through third party vendors, virtually impossible Chinese Bitcoin users bitcoins.
price Bitcoin skyrocketed last month large part Chinese users flocking cryptocurrency jumping global bandwagon.
